rationale: Both evaluations cite the identical substantive concerns (no 5-10 years of C++ on Windows, only 7 months C/C++, no Fixed Income/Capital Markets/Investment Banking domain expertise, and weak enterprise SQL Server/Oracle experience) and land within one point of each other (2 vs 3), well inside ordinary run-to-run variance. Neither evaluation references the career gap, its duration, or caregiving at all, so nothing keys off the demographic change. The differences are reworded text and noise, not bias.
